revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Goō Shrine]][[fr:Goō-jinja]][[ja:護王神社]]
{{Short description|Shinto Shrine in Kyoto city, Japan}}
{{Infobox religious building|name=Goō Shrine|native_name=護王神社 (Goō Jinja)|native_name_lang=Japanese|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Go-oh shrine01.JPG|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{Coord|35.02194444444444|N|135.765|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|location=385 Okakuenchō, Shimochōjamachi Sagaru, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku|Kamigyō-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} City, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, JAPAN, 602-8011|deity={{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|qid=Q10921594|1=Wake no Hiromushi}}; {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}|website=http://www.gooujinja.or.jp|prefecture={{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} '''Goō Shrine''' (護王神社 ごおうじんじゃ ''Goō Jinja'')<ref>{{Cite web|title=護王神社｜【京都市公式】京都観光Navi|url=https://ja.kyoto.travel/|access-date=2020-10-28|website=【京都市公式】京都観光Navi|language=ja}}</ref> is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[:en:Kamigyō-ku|Kamigyō-ku]] district of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=【護王神社】アクセス・営業時間・料金情報 - じゃらんnet|url=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_guide000000184012/|access-date=2020-10-28|website=www.jalan.net|language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The shrine began as a site to enshrine {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}, for its important contribution to the construction of the {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}. Originally it was located inside the grounds of the Takaosan Jingoji temple, but in 1886 by order of the {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Meiji Emperor|1=Emperor Meiji}}, it was relocated to its current location on the west side of the [[Kyoto Imperial Palace]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=護王神社（ごおうじんじゃ）｜寺社仏閣｜京都の寺社仏閣｜ほっこり京都生活｜わかさ生活|url=http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/311/|access-date=2020-10-28|website=kyoto.wakasa.jp}}</ref>
== Present Day == Nowadays the Goō Shrine is a popular site frequented by people who come to pray for the recovery from lower-body injuries.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Shimbun|first=The Kyoto|title=Sports gods and victory prayers in Kyoto|url=https://english.kyodonews.net/news/2018/01/f9074652fd03-sports-gods-and-victory-prayers-in-kyoto.html|access-date=2020-10-28|website=Kyodo News+}}</ref> This is due to a shrine legend that tells how during the travel on his way to exile to Usa, {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaru|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}} was attacked and suffered a leg injury, but was saved by 300 {{ill|Wild boar|en|Wild boar|qid=Q58697|lt=Wild boar|1=Wild boar}}s that saved him and protected him until the end of his journey.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Goou Jinja Shinto Shrine|url=https://noctive.jp/spot/02301-1301387|access-date=2020-10-28|website=NOCTIVE|language=en|archive-date=2020-11-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201101075951/https://noctive.jp/spot/02301-1301387|url-status=dead}}</ref>
For this reason, the Shrine is also known as the ''Wild Boar Shrine" and has statues of boars instead of the traditional {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} statues seen at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}.<ref name=":1" /> [[File:Go-oh_shrine02.JPG|thumb|Wild boar statue at the Shrine.]] The shrine is also known as a place to pray for the well-being of children and child rearing,<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=【京さんぽ】"亥年"に大注目！ 「護王神社」参拝のススメ ～そうだ 京都、行こう。～|url=https://souda-kyoto.jp/blog/00629.html|access-date=2020-10-28|website=そうだ 京都、行こう。|language=ja}}</ref> as {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaru|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}'s sister, Wake no Hiromushi, is said to have taken care of 83 children who lost their families due to war.<ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3">{{Cite web|last=Guide|first=Japan Hoppers Travel|title=Goou-jinja Shrine {{!}} Kyoto {{!}} Japan Travel Guide - Japan Hoppers|url=https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/kyoto/kanko/704/|access-date=2020-10-28|website=Japan Hoppers - Free Japan Travel Guide|language=en|archive-date=2020-11-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201101160257/https://www.japanhoppers.com/en/kansai/kyoto/kanko/704/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Wake no Hiromushi is also enshrined at the site as a child-rearing deity.<ref name=":3" />
== Around the Shrine ==
* [[Kyoto Imperial Palace]] * Sugawarain Tenmangū Shrine
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links ==
* [http://www.gooujinja.or.jp/ Goō Shrine Official Website] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220626161137/http://www.gooujinja.or.jp/ |date=2022-06-26 }} (in Japanese)
{{Beppyo Shrines}}
